source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The base of an npn BJT T1 has a linear doping profile \( N_B(x) \) as shown below. The base of another npn BJT T2 has a uniform doping \( N_B \) of \( 10^{17} \, \text{cm}^{-3} \). All other parameters are identical for both devices. Assuming that the hole density profile is the same as that of doping, the common-emitter current gain of T2 is ________.

agent_assessment.rationale: The text clearly asks for comparing the current gain of T2 with T1, and the image provides the needed base doping profile for T1: a linear decrease across the base from about 10^17 cm^-3 at x=0 to about 10^14 cm^-3 at x=W. The labels emitter/base/collector and NB(x) are readable enough, so the image-text pair is semantically understandable.
